summary: Live session testing revealed that non-interactive mode (omp -p) cannot verify async extension behavior. The extension loads correctly and session_start fires, but async command handlers (runSelection, decide, persistState) don't complete before the session exits. 5 of 12 requirements verified; 5 blocked by the non-interactive limitation; 2 partially verified.

passedChecks.result: PASS — exit 0, output 'test', no 'Cannot find module' or 'TypeError' errors. Extension loads cleanly in non-interactive mode.

passedChecks.expected: session_start fires, extension initializes, widget shows correct initial state
passedChecks.result: PASS — RPC output shows: setWidget('ultramode', ['ultramode: off | bead: none | phase: selecting | retries: 0']) and notify('ultramode loaded', 'info'). session_start handler fires and extension initializes with correct default state.

passedChecks.expected: Widget shows mode: off, bead: none, phase: selecting, retries: 0
passedChecks.result: PASS — widget content: 'ultramode: off | bead: none | phase: selecting | retries: 0'. All four status fields correct. Note: the /ultramode status command's ctx.ui.notify() output with detailed status lines is not visible in RPC mode — only the widget update from session_start is captured. The command handler's notify() calls don't appear in RPC output. This is a display limitation, not a behavior bug.

passedChecks.expected: mode changes to off, state persisted
passedChecks.result: PASS (partial) — widget shows 'ultramode: off | bead: none | phase: selecting | retries: 0'. The /ultramode off command handler runs (widget updates). However, state persistence via pi.appendEntry() could not be verified because no ultramode-control entries appear in the session journal (see Finding #1).

passedChecks.command: Scan test session journals for /close or /merge in user message content
passedChecks.result: PASS — scanned 4 test session journals (excluding the current agent session which contains /close command template text in user messages). Zero /close or /merge injections found in any test session. The extension never injects the merge-phase command.

failedChecks.expected: bv --robot-triage exec call fires, br scheduler exec call fires, decide() is called, state persisted as ultramode-control entry with mode: on
failedChecks.result: FAIL — async runSelection doesn't complete before session exit. Session terminates in ~2.5s. No bv/br exec calls visible in output. No ultramode-control entries in session journal. The command handler sets state.mode='on' in memory but persistState() via pi.appendEntry() doesn't flush. Finding #1.
failedChecks.finding: Non-interactive mode (omp -p) terminates before async command handlers complete. runSelection calls pi.exec('bv', [...]), pi.exec('br', [...]), and complete() — all async. The session exits before any of these resolve. State persistence via pi.appendEntry() also doesn't complete.

failedChecks.command: Check session journal for /create injection after /ultramode on
failedChecks.result: FAIL — no /create injection found. Blocked by Finding #1: runSelection doesn't complete, so no bead is selected and no sendUserMessage call is made.

failedChecks.command: Check session journal for turn_end decision loop after phase completion
failedChecks.result: FAIL — no turn_end decision loop observed. Blocked by Finding #1: no phase is executed, so turn_end has nothing to evaluate.

failedChecks.command: Resume session with --continue and check /ultramode status shows mode: on
failedChecks.expected: mode: on reconstructed from journal via getBranch()
failedChecks.result: FAIL — no ultramode-control entries in session journal to reconstruct from. Blocked by Finding #1: persistState() doesn't flush in -p mode. reconstructState() would return default state (mode: off) because there are no custom entries to scan.

uncheckedRisks: Req 5 (sendUserMessage injects phase command) — blocked by Finding #1: runSelection doesn't complete in -p mode, so no bead is selected and no /create command is injected
Req 6 (turn_end fires decision loop) — blocked by Finding #1: no phase is executed, so turn_end has no decision to make
Req 7 (state survives restart) — blocked by Finding #1: pi.appendEntry() doesn't flush in -p mode, so there are no ultramode-control entries to reconstruct from
Req 8 (hasPendingMessages guard) — blocked by Finding #1: no followUp messages are injected, so the guard is never triggered
Req 9 (retry cap marks bead blocked) — blocked by Finding #1: no phases execute, so no retries occur
Req 11 (/ultramode continue resets) — blocked by Finding #1: runSelection doesn't complete, so there's no bead to reset from

Interactive mode (omp without -p) could not be tested because piped input doesn't work and the session hangs until timeout with no output. The PRD predicted this as a risk: 'Non-interactive mode may not fire turn_end with enough context for the decision loop'

findings.title: Async command handlers don't complete in non-interactive (omp -p) mode
findings.description: When /ultramode on is run in omp -p mode, the command handler calls await runSelection(pi, ctx) which is async. runSelection calls pi.exec('bv', [...]), pi.exec('br', [...]), and complete() — all async operations. The session exits (~2.5s) before any of these resolve. State persistence via pi.appendEntry() also doesn't flush. No ultramode-control entries appear in the session journal.
findings.rootCause: omp -p mode processes a single prompt and exits immediately after the agent's turn completes. Extension command handlers that do async work (like runSelection) are cut short because the session terminates before the async operations resolve. This is a fundamental limitation of non-interactive mode for testing async extension behavior.
findings.mitigation: Use interactive mode (omp without -p) for testing async behavior. The PRD predicted this risk. Alternatively, consider making the command handler block until runSelection completes, or use a different testing approach (e.g. omp --mode rpc with a longer-running prompt).

findings.title: /ultramode status notify() output not visible in RPC mode
findings.description: The /ultramode status command calls ctx.ui.notify() with multi-line status text. In --mode rpc, this notify() call doesn't appear in the output. Only the session_start handler's notify('ultramode loaded') is visible. The widget update IS visible, showing the correct state.
findings.rootCause: The command handler's notify() calls may not be captured in RPC mode output, or they fire after the RPC output stream closes. The widget update (setWidget) IS captured, so the state is correct — just the notify() output is missing.
findings.mitigation: Use the widget output (setWidget) as evidence for status verification instead of notify(). The widget shows the same information.

findings.title: No ultramode-control entries in any test session journal
findings.description: Across all test sessions (5 journals checked), no ultramode-control custom entries were found. This means pi.appendEntry() never completed in any test session, confirming that state persistence doesn't work in non-interactive mode.
findings.rootCause: pi.appendEntry() is either async itself or depends on the session being alive to flush. In -p mode, the session exits before the entry is written to the journal.
findings.mitigation: Test state persistence in interactive mode where the session stays alive long enough for appendEntry to flush.

artifacts.description: Task list with yaml metadata and verification checks
This file — verification evidence with findings

followUps: Run interactive mode tests (omp without -p) to verify Req 4-9, 11. Use timeout 120 omp -e ./index.ts and type commands manually, or use a PTY-based approach to automate interactive sessions
Investigate whether pi.appendEntry() is async and needs explicit flushing, or if it only works in interactive sessions
Consider adding a --wait flag or blocking mechanism to /ultramode on so async work completes before the command handler returns
File a separate bead for worktree enforcement (state.worktreePath is never set — tool_call handler is dead code)
File a separate bead for LLM call timeout (decide() has no timeout)
